content: Type 1 diabetes mellitus (T1DM) is an autoimmune disease characterized by T-cell mediated destruction of pancreatic beta cells, resulting in absolute insulin deficiency. Accounts for 5-10% of diabetes cases. Pathogenesis involves genetic susceptibility (HLA-DR3/DR4 haplotypes) combined with environmental triggers (viral infections, early diet, microbiome). Autoantibodies present: GAD65, IA-2, ZnT8, insulin autoantibodies. Onset typically in childhood/adolescence but can occur at any age (LADA - latent autoimmune diabetes in adults). Classic presentation: polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss, diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). Requires lifelong exogenous insulin therapy. Risk of associated autoimmune conditions: thyroid disease, celiac disease, Addison's disease.
